description: Set up project infrastructure: install dependencies (better-sqlite3 for persistent storage, resend for email), configure the database layer with the 2-table schema (users, medications), and create shared TypeScript types.
steps: Run eval "$(mise activate bash)" before any node/npm commands to get Node 22 in PATH
Install dependencies: npm install better-sqlite3 resend && npm install -D @types/better-sqlite3

Create lib/db.ts - Initialize SQLite database at ./data/health-reminder.db. Create the 2 tables: users (id, email, timezone, daily_water_goal, water_consumed_today, last_water_log_date) and medications (id, user_id, name, reminder_times as JSON text, last_taken_date). Insert a default demo user (id=1, email='demo@example.com', timezone='America/New_York', daily_water_goal=2000, water_consumed_today=0, last_water_log_date=null).
Create lib/types.ts - Define TypeScript interfaces: User (matching users table), Medication (matching medications table with reminder_times as string[]), CreateMedicationInput, UpdateWaterInput.
Create a .gitignore entry to ignore data/*.db files
Read node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route.md before proceeding to confirm route handler patterns
acceptance_criteria: npm run build succeeds with the new files
lib/db.ts exports a getDb() function that returns a configured SQLite database instance
lib/types.ts exports User and Medication interfaces matching the PRD schema
The database is created with both tables on first access
verification: eval "$(mise activate bash)" && cd /projects/sandbox/nextjs-medication-reminder && npm run build
findings: Build and lint pass cleanly with Next.js 16.2.4. better-sqlite3 and resend installed successfully. The database uses WAL mode for better concurrency. The lib/types.ts Medication interface uses reminder_times: string[] (parsed from JSON text column). Node 22 via mise works correctly.
